changes_made: Changed k8s_node_failure_tolerant_setup to k8s_node_failure_tolerance_configured for consistency and clarity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to the NIST SI-13(5) control, focusing on predictable failure prevention and failover capabilities. The naming conventions mostly follow the prescribed pattern, but a minor change was made to ensure consistency and clarity.
naming_improvements: Ensure consistent use of 'configured' for setup-related checks to align with existing naming patterns.
title: Predictable Failure Prevention - Predictable failure prevention | failover capability provide [ selection
step1_reasoning: The control SI-13(5) focuses on predictable failure prevention and failover capabilities. In Kubernetes, ensuring high availability and failover capabilities can be achieved through several mechanisms. Configuring etcd backups ensures data resilience. Setting up the API server in a high-availability configuration prevents single points of failure. Configuring liveness probes for pods ensures that failing pods are detected and restarted. Ensuring node failure tolerance involves setting up node pools and using features like PodDisruptionBudgets.
